tools.name: health_check
tools.description: Use this when checking whether an MCP-compatible client can reach the KimchiSushi server.

tools.name: get_kimchisushi_agent_entrypoints
tools.description: Use this when routing a Korea/Japan question to KimchiSushi free, study, working-holiday, guide, or citation-source pages.

tools.name: search
tools.description: Use this when searching KimchiSushi for source-backed Korea/Japan study, working-holiday, or public community information. Returns citation-ready URLs.

tools.name: fetch
tools.description: Use this after search to fetch one KimchiSushi citation source or public community post by its opaque result id.

tools.name: get_recent_posts
tools.description: Use this when the user wants recent or popular public KimchiSushi community posts without a keyword search. Returns only content visible to site visitors.

tools.name: get_post_with_comments
tools.description: Use this when fetching one known public KimchiSushi post and its public comments by UUID. Korean and Japanese fields are translated mirrors.

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "search" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "fetch" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.

findings.title: Toxic flow: untrusted input can reach an external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: search, fetch, get_recent_posts, get_post_with_comments · Public sink: fetch, get_post_with_comments.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: search, fetch, get_recent_posts, get_post_with_comments · Public sink: fetch, get_post_with_comments

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
